Rice___Blast.disease_description: Rice Blast is a serious fungal disease caused by Magnaporthe oryzae. It mainly attacks leaves, nodes, and panicles. Diamond-shaped lesions with gray or white centers and brown margins appear on leaves, reducing photosynthesis and grain yield.
Rice___Blast.cure_and_management: The best fungicide is Tricyclazole or Azoxystrobin. Spray Tricyclazole at 0.6 grams per litre of water. Apply during early disease appearance. Maintain proper water level and avoid excess nitrogen fertilizer.
Rice___Blast.usage_method: Mix the recommended dose in clean water and spray uniformly on leaves in the evening hours. Repeat after 10–12 days if infection persists.

Rice___Brown_Spot.disease_description: Brown Spot disease is caused by Bipolaris oryzae fungus and is common in nutrient-deficient soils. Circular brown spots with yellow halos appear on leaves, leading to leaf drying and poor grain quality.
Rice___Brown_Spot.cure_and_management: Mancozeb is the most effective fungicide. Apply Mancozeb at 2 grams per litre of water. Balanced fertilization with nitrogen and potassium reduces disease severity.
Rice___Brown_Spot.usage_method: Spray the fungicide evenly on both sides of leaves. Two sprays at 10-day intervals give good control.

Wheat___Rust.disease_description: Wheat Rust is a fungal disease caused by Puccinia species. It produces orange or brown powdery pustules on leaves and stems, reducing grain filling and yield.
Wheat___Rust.cure_and_management: Propiconazole or Tebuconazole fungicides are effective. Growing resistant wheat varieties is the best long-term solution.
Wheat___Rust.usage_method: Spray Propiconazole at 1 ml per litre of water when pustules first appear. One or two sprays are sufficient.

Maize___Leaf_Blight.disease_description: Leaf Blight of maize is caused by Helminthosporium turcicum. Large tan or brown lesions start from leaf edges and spread rapidly under humid conditions.
Maize___Leaf_Blight.cure_and_management: Mancozeb or Propiconazole fungicide controls the disease. Proper drainage and crop rotation help prevent infection.
Maize___Leaf_Blight.usage_method: Spray Mancozeb at 2 g per litre during early disease stage. Repeat spray after 12 days if required.

Tomato___Late_Blight.disease_description: Late Blight is a destructive disease caused by Phytophthora infestans. Dark water-soaked lesions appear on leaves and fruits, followed by white fungal growth, leading to plant death.
Tomato___Late_Blight.cure_and_management: Metalaxyl or Mancozeb fungicides are highly effective. Avoid overhead irrigation and ensure proper air circulation.
Tomato___Late_Blight.usage_method: Spray Metalaxyl at recommended dose in cool evening hours. Repeat every 7–10 days during wet weather.

Tomato___Leaf_Curl.disease_description: Tomato Leaf Curl Virus is spread by whiteflies. Infected plants show upward leaf curling, yellowing, and stunted growth, causing severe yield loss.
Tomato___Leaf_Curl.cure_and_management: Imidacloprid insecticide controls whitefly population. Use yellow sticky traps to monitor insects.
Tomato___Leaf_Curl.usage_method: Spray Imidacloprid at 0.3 ml per litre on lower leaf surfaces. Apply early morning or evening.

Potato___Late_Blight.disease_description: Potato Late Blight causes rapid browning of leaves and stems, followed by tuber rot. The disease spreads quickly in cool and moist conditions.
Potato___Late_Blight.cure_and_management: Metalaxyl or Ridomil Gold is recommended. Timely spraying prevents complete crop loss.
Potato___Late_Blight.usage_method: Spray Ridomil Gold at 2 g per litre of water. Two to three sprays at 7-day intervals are effective.

Brinjal___Fruit_Borer.disease_description: Fruit and Shoot Borer is a major insect pest. Larvae bore into shoots and fruits, causing wilting and making fruits unfit for sale.
Brinjal___Fruit_Borer.cure_and_management: Spinosad or Emamectin Benzoate gives excellent control. Remove and destroy affected shoots regularly.
Brinjal___Fruit_Borer.usage_method: Spray Emamectin Benzoate at 0.4 g per litre of water. Apply at 10-day intervals.

Mustard___Alternaria_Blight.disease_description: Alternaria Blight is caused by Alternaria brassicae. It forms circular dark brown spots on leaves and pods, reducing oil content and seed quality.
Mustard___Alternaria_Blight.cure_and_management: Mancozeb fungicide is effective. Use disease-free seeds and proper spacing.
Mustard___Alternaria_Blight.usage_method: Spray Mancozeb at 2 g per litre at first symptom appearance and repeat after 12 days.
